items.title: Missing security headers
items.description: Check responses for missing or weak security headers: HSTS, Content-Security-Policy, X-Content-Type-Options, X-Frame-Options and Referrer-Policy. A fast signal of how well the app is hardened overall.
items.examples:
curl -skI https://target.com | grep -iE 'strict-transport|content-security|x-frame|x-content-type'
nikto -h https://target.com
Paste the host into securityheaders.com

items.title: Insecure cookie flags
items.description: Inspect Set-Cookie headers on session and auth cookies for HttpOnly, Secure and SameSite. Missing flags expose tokens to theft via XSS or leakage, and weaken CSRF defences.
items.examples:
curl -skI https://target.com/login | grep -i set-cookie
Browser DevTools > Application > Cookies

items.title: Exposed sensitive files
items.description: Probe for files that should not be public: .git/, .env, backups, robots.txt entries, server-status and framework consoles. Quick to check and often high impact.
items.examples:
curl -s https://target.com/.git/HEAD
ffuf -u https://target.com/FUZZ -w SecLists/Discovery/Web-Content/common.txt -mc 200
nuclei -t exposures -u https://target.com

items.title: Verbose errors / debug mode
items.description: Trigger error conditions and look for stack traces, framework debug pages, version banners or internal paths in responses. These leak information that fuels later attacks.
items.examples:
curl -sk "https://target.com/?id=%27"
Send malformed JSON to an API endpoint
curl -skI https://target.com | grep -iE 'server|x-powered-by'

items.title: Weak authentication
items.description: Assess login security app-wide: password policy, brute-force/lockout protection, MFA on sensitive roles, username enumeration and default or shared accounts. Maps to OWASP A07.
items.examples:
hydra -l admin -P rockyou.txt target.com http-post-form "/login:user=^USER^&pass=^PASS^:invalid"
Compare login error messages for valid vs invalid users
Fire 100 bad logins, watch for lockout

items.title: Session management flaws
items.description: Review session token strength, fixation, lifetime, logout invalidation and reuse after a password change. Tokens should rotate on login and be killed on logout.
items.examples:
Capture tokens in Burp; reuse one after logout
Burp Sequencer for token entropy
Check if the pre-login token survives authentication (fixation)

items.title: Cryptographic / TLS failures
items.description: Check transport and data protection: TLS versions and ciphers, certificate validity, HTTP-to-HTTPS redirection, and sensitive data sent or stored in clear. Maps to OWASP A02.
items.examples:
testssl.sh https://target.com
sslscan target.com
nmap --script ssl-enum-ciphers -p 443 target.com

items.title: Vulnerable components
items.description: Fingerprint server, framework and library versions and map them to known CVEs. Outdated components are a frequent breach entry point. Maps to OWASP A06.
items.examples:
whatweb https://target.com
nmap -sV --script vuln target.com
nuclei -t cves -u https://target.com
retire.js against the app's JavaScript

items.title: Business logic flaws
items.description: Hunt logic abuse scanners miss: workflow/step bypass, price or quantity tampering, race conditions, request replay and skipped approval steps. Maps to OWASP A04 (Insecure Design).
items.examples:
Replay a purchase in Burp Repeater with a tampered total
Set a negative quantity or price
Turbo Intruder to race a coupon redemption

items.title: Missing rate limiting
items.description: Confirm sensitive actions (login, OTP, password reset, costly API calls) enforce rate limits. Their absence enables brute force, enumeration and resource-exhaustion abuse.
items.examples:
ffuf -u https://target.com/api/login -w passwords.txt -d '{"user":"a","pass":"FUZZ"}' -mc all
Watch for 429 / Retry-After responses under bursts

items.title: Software/data integrity
items.description: Check for unsigned updates, unsafe deserialization, CI/CD or dependency trust gaps, and third-party scripts loaded without Subresource Integrity. Maps to OWASP A08.
items.examples:
Review <script src> tags for missing integrity= attributes
Inspect any auto-update mechanism
ysoserial to craft deserialization PoCs

items.title: Logging & monitoring gaps
items.description: Assess whether security events (logins, failures, access denials) are logged and alertable and whether an attack would be noticed. Hard to prove externally; confirm with the client. Maps to OWASP A09.
items.examples:
Ask whether failed logins are logged and alerted
Look for audit trails on sensitive actions
Note any absence of lockout or anomaly alerting

items.title: IDOR / broken object access
items.description: For object references this endpoint exposes, swap in other users' IDs and tamper identifiers to reach data you should not. The core of Broken Access Control. Maps to OWASP A01.
items.examples:
curl https://target.com/api/orders/1002 -H 'Cookie: <userA-session>'
Burp Autorize extension
Swap sequential IDs and UUIDs in the request

items.title: Missing function-level authz
items.description: Test whether this endpoint enforces role checks. Hit it unauthenticated and as a low-privileged user, attempt admin-only actions, and try forced browsing to it.
items.examples:
Replay an admin request using a normal user's cookie
curl -X POST https://target.com/admin/users   # unauthenticated
Burp Autorize across roles

items.title: SQL injection
items.description: Inject SQL metacharacters and payloads into this endpoint's parameters, headers and body. Watch for database errors, boolean/time-based differences or data leakage. Part of OWASP A03.
items.examples:
sqlmap -u 'https://target.com/item?id=1' --cookie='<session>' --batch
Manual: ' OR 1=1-- -
Time-based: 1 AND SLEEP(5)

items.title: OS command injection
items.description: Where input on this endpoint may reach a shell, inject command separators and confirm execution via output or response timing. Part of OWASP A03.
items.examples:
Payloads: ; id   | whoami   $(sleep 5)
ffuf with a command-injection wordlist
Confirm blind cases out-of-band with interactsh

items.title: Path traversal / LFI
items.description: On endpoints taking a file or path parameter, use directory-traversal sequences to read files outside the web root or include local files.
items.examples:
curl 'https://target.com/download?f=../../../../etc/passwd'
Encoded: ..%2f..%2f
ffuf with an LFI/traversal wordlist

items.title: Cross-site scripting (XSS)
items.description: Inject script payloads into this endpoint's parameters and stored fields. Check reflection in HTML, attribute and JS contexts and whether the payload executes.
items.examples:
Payload: "><script>alert(1)</script>
dalfox url https://target.com/?q=FUZZ
Verify stored payloads in the rendered page

items.title: CSRF protection missing
items.description: For state-changing requests on this endpoint, check for an unpredictable anti-CSRF token and SameSite cookies. Replay the action cross-origin without the token.
items.examples:
Remove or replay the CSRF token in Burp Repeater
Build a test HTML form auto-posting to the endpoint
Check SameSite on the session cookie

items.title: Server-side request forgery
items.description: Where this endpoint fetches a URL or resource, point it at internal addresses or an out-of-band host to prove the server makes attacker-controlled requests. Maps to OWASP A10.
items.examples:
url=http://169.254.169.254/latest/meta-data/
url=http://<interactsh-id>.oast.fun
Test open-redirect chains and DNS rebinding
